description: Make MCP workflow guidance accessible to AI agents regardless of client capabilities. The MCP server has comprehensive instructions, prompts, and resources for tasks like plugin development, but they rely on three client-dependent delivery mechanisms: (1) the `instructions` field in InitializeResult (spec says clients MAY add it to the system prompt — not MUST), (2) MCP prompts that are user-initiated only (AI cannot call prompts/get autonomously), and (3) MCP resources that require the client to expose resources/read. When a client does not surface these, the AI has zero workflow context, leading to guessing, asking the user questions, and poor results. This PRD adds three fallback mechanisms so the workflow guidance reaches AI agents through channels they always receive: CLAUDE.md (file-based context), callable tools (not prompts), and enhanced tool descriptions.

userStories.title: Add MCP workflow triggers section to root CLAUDE.md
userStories.description: Add a new section to CLAUDE.md that mirrors the Workflow Triggers table from SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS, so that AI agents receiving file-based context (which is reliable) know to invoke MCP prompts for specific tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md contains a '## MCP Workflow Triggers' section (or similar heading) after the 'You Are Connected to This Project's MCP Server' subsection
The section lists all 6 workflow triggers with their corresponding prompt names: build_plugin, troubleshoot, setup_plugin, plugin_icon, audit_ai_docs, contribute_learnings
The section explains that these are MCP prompts available via slash commands (e.g., /build_plugin) AND via the new plugin_get_workflow tool
The section includes the list of MCP resource URIs that can be fetched via resources/read
The section emphasizes that the build_plugin prompt MUST be invoked before attempting to build a plugin — do not build from general knowledge
userStories.notes:
Files:
- CLAUDE.md (add new section)

Context:
The root CLAUDE.md is loaded as file-based context by AI clients at session start. It currently describes the MCP server connection (lines 40-51) and lists available tools (extension_get_state, browser_*, plugin tools, etc.) but does NOT mention MCP prompts, resources, or workflow triggers. The SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS in `platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts` (lines 353-378) contain a Workflow Triggers table and Resources list that is sent via the MCP `instructions` field — but the MCP spec says clients MAY add instructions to the system prompt, so delivery is not guaranteed.

Changes:
Add a new subsection after line 51 (after 'When the user says "reload the extension"...') and before '### Tech Stack' (line 53). The new section should include:

1. A paragraph explaining that the MCP server provides prompts and resources containing accumulated workflow patterns from previous AI sessions
2. The workflow triggers table (mirroring lines 357-364 of mcp-setup.ts):
   - Build/create plugin → build_plugin(url, name?) prompt OR plugin_get_workflow tool
   - Troubleshoot → troubleshoot(error?)
   - Install/setup plugin → setup_plugin(name)
   - Add/update icon → plugin_icon(plugin)
   - Audit AI docs → audit_ai_docs
   - After any task → contribute_learnings(task?)
3. A note that prompts can be invoked as slash commands or the AI can call `plugin_get_workflow` tool directly
4. The resource URI list from lines 370-378 of mcp-setup.ts
5. A strong emphasis: 'When asked to build a plugin, you MUST invoke the build_plugin prompt or call plugin_get_workflow first. These contain critical site analysis steps, API discovery patterns, auth detection techniques, and common gotchas. Do NOT attempt to build a plugin from general knowledge.'

Constraints:
- Keep the section concise — CLAUDE.md instructions say 'Keep additions high-level — avoid excessive detail that wastes context'
- Do NOT duplicate the full prompt content — just reference it and explain how to access it
- Do NOT list individual plugins by name (CLAUDE.md convention)
- The section should be plugin-agnostic, describing the pattern not specific plugins

userStories.title: Add plugin_get_workflow callable tool to MCP server
userStories.description: Create a new platform tool `plugin_get_workflow` that returns the full build_plugin prompt text and embedded resource content. Unlike MCP prompts (which are user-initiated slash commands), tools can be called autonomously by the AI agent. This gives AI agents a reliable way to access the plugin development workflow without depending on the client to support prompts/get.
acceptanceCriteria: A new tool named `plugin_get_workflow` appears in the MCP tools/list response
Calling plugin_get_workflow with a `url` argument returns the full build_plugin prompt text
The response includes embedded content from opentabs://guide/plugin-development and opentabs://reference/sdk-api resources
The tool accepts optional `name` and `workflow` arguments (workflow defaults to 'build_plugin')
The tool is registered as a platform tool (bypasses permissions, always available, hidden from side panel)
The tool supports at minimum the build_plugin workflow; other workflows (troubleshoot, setup_plugin) can be added later
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts (add tool to PLATFORM_TOOLS array and handler in CallToolRequestSchema)
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.test.ts (update test that checks handler count and platform tool count)

Context:
Platform tools are defined in the `PLATFORM_TOOLS` array at line 126 of mcp-setup.ts. They are raw JSON Schema objects (not Zod-based like browser tools). The CallToolRequestSchema handler at line 240 dispatches platform tools by name before checking browser tools and plugin tools. The tool should reuse the existing prompt resolvers from `mcp-prompts.ts`.

The `resolvePrompt` function in `mcp-prompts.ts` (line 211) returns a `PromptResult` with `{ description, messages }`. Each message has `{ role, content }` where content is either `{ type: 'text', text }` or `{ type: 'resource', resource: { uri, mimeType, text } }`. The tool handler needs to flatten these into a single text response suitable for a tools/call result.

Changes:
1. Add a new entry to PLATFORM_TOOLS (after `plugin_mark_reviewed` at line 169):
   ```
   {
     name: 'plugin_get_workflow',
     description: 'Get a complete workflow guide for an OpenTabs task. Returns step-by-step instructions with patterns, code templates, and common gotchas accumulated from previous AI sessions. Call this before attempting to build a plugin, troubleshoot an issue, or set up a plugin — the workflow contains critical information not available from general knowledge. Available workflows: build_plugin (default), troubleshoot, setup_plugin.',
     inputSchema: {
       type: 'object',
       properties: {
         workflow: { type: 'string', enum: ['build_plugin', 'troubleshoot', 'setup_plugin'], description: 'Which workflow to retrieve (default: build_plugin)' },
         url: { type: 'string', description: 'Target web app URL (required for build_plugin workflow)' },
         name: { type: 'string', description: 'Plugin name in kebab-case (optional, for build_plugin workflow)' },
         error: { type: 'string', description: 'Error message to diagnose (optional, for troubleshoot workflow)' },
       },
     },
   }
   ```

2. Add handler in CallToolRequestSchema (after the `plugin_mark_reviewed` check at line 249):
   ```
   if (toolName === 'plugin_get_workflow') {
     return handlePluginGetWorkflow(args);
   }
   ```

3. Implement `handlePluginGetWorkflow(args)` as a local function. It should:
   - Import `resolvePrompt` from `./mcp-prompts.js`
   - Determine which prompt to resolve based on `args.workflow` (default 'build_plugin')
   - Map workflow to prompt args: build_plugin → { url, name }, troubleshoot → { error }, setup_plugin → { name: args.name }
   - Call `resolvePrompt(promptName, promptArgs)`
   - Flatten the PromptResult messages into a single text string: concatenate text content and resource content blocks
   - Return `{ content: [{ type: 'text', text: flattenedText }] }`

4. Add 'plugin_get_workflow' to the `PLATFORM_TOOL_NAMES` set (it's auto-derived from PLATFORM_TOOLS at line 173, so just adding to the array is sufficient)

5. Update SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS Workflow Triggers table to mention the tool as an alternative:
   Current (line 355): 'When the user's request matches a pattern below, you **MUST** invoke the corresponding prompt before proceeding — do not attempt the task from general knowledge.'
   Add: mention that AI agents can also call `plugin_get_workflow` tool directly if prompt invocation is not available.

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify the existing prompt resolvers — reuse them as-is
- Do NOT add this as a browser tool — it's a platform tool (no browser dispatch needed)
- The handler must NOT require ServerState (prompts are stateless) — or if it does need state for resource resolution, accept state as a parameter
- Keep the response as plain text, not JSON — the AI agent reads it as instructions

Traps:
- `resolvePrompt` in mcp-prompts.ts returns `PromptMessage[]` where content can be TextContent or EmbeddedResourceContent. The handler must handle both content types. EmbeddedResourceContent has `resource.text` (the actual content) and `resource.uri` (the source URI). Flatten by joining all text segments with section headers.
- `getStaticResourceContent(uri)` in mcp-resources.ts (line 173) returns static content without needing state — use this if you need resources independently of the prompt resolver.
- The test at line 1074 of mcp-setup.test.ts checks 'registers exactly 7 handlers' — this count should NOT change since we're adding to PLATFORM_TOOLS (handled by the existing CallToolRequestSchema handler), not adding a new handler. But the test that counts platform tools may need updating.

userStories.title: Enhance plugin_analyze_site tool description with workflow guidance
userStories.description: Update the plugin_analyze_site tool description to include a brief workflow reminder, since tool descriptions are the one piece of context that ALL MCP clients always expose to AI agents (via tools/list). When an AI sees this tool in its tool list, the description should nudge it toward the full workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: plugin_analyze_site description includes a note like 'This is Phase 2 of the plugin development workflow. Call plugin_get_workflow first for the complete guide.'
The description addition is concise (1-2 sentences) and does not bloat the tool description
The existing description content is preserved — only appended to
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/analyze-site.ts (update description field)

Context:
The `plugin_analyze_site` tool is defined at line 17 of analyze-site.ts via `defineBrowserTool`. Its current description (lines 19-31) is a comprehensive multi-line string covering what the tool does, what it detects, and what it returns.

Changes:
Append to the end of the existing description string:
`'\n\nThis tool is Phase 2 of the plugin development workflow. For the complete step-by-step guide (including auth discovery, API mapping, scaffolding, implementation patterns, and common gotchas), call the plugin_get_workflow tool first.'`

Constraints:
- Do NOT rewrite the existing description — only append
- Keep the addition to 1-2 sentences maximum
- Reference plugin_get_workflow by name so the AI can find it in its tool list

userStories.title: Update SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS to reference plugin_get_workflow as a fallback
userStories.description: Update the Workflow Triggers section of SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS to mention that AI agents can call the plugin_get_workflow tool directly as an alternative to invoking prompts, ensuring the guidance is accessible even when the client does not support MCP prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS Workflow Triggers section mentions plugin_get_workflow as an alternative for build_plugin, troubleshoot, and setup_plugin workflows
The instructions clarify that prompts (slash commands) are preferred but the tool is available as a fallback
The existing instruction 'you MUST invoke the corresponding prompt' is updated to say 'you MUST invoke the corresponding prompt OR call plugin_get_workflow'
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts (update SERVER_INSTRUCTIONS constant, lines 353-364)

Context:
The current Workflow Triggers section at lines 353-364 says:
```
## Workflow Triggers

When the user's request matches a pattern below, you **MUST** invoke the corresponding prompt before proceeding — do not attempt the task from general knowledge. These prompts contain accumulated patterns, gotchas, and workflows from previous AI sessions that are critical for success.

| User intent | Prompt to invoke | Also fetch these resources |
|---|---|---|
| Build / create / develop a plugin | `build_plugin(url, name?)` | `opentabs://guide/plugin-development`, `opentabs://reference/sdk-api` |
...
```

Changes:
1. Update the introductory paragraph (line 355) to:
   'When the user's request matches a pattern below, you **MUST** invoke the corresponding prompt before proceeding. If prompt invocation is not available, call the `plugin_get_workflow` tool instead — it returns the same workflow content. Do not attempt the task from general knowledge. These workflows contain accumulated patterns, gotchas, and techniques from previous AI sessions that are critical for success.'

2. Add a row or note to the table mentioning plugin_get_workflow as the tool alternative. For example, add a note below the table:
   'If your client does not support MCP prompts, call `plugin_get_workflow(workflow: "build_plugin", url: "...")` to get the same content as a tool response.'

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove the existing prompt references — the tool is a fallback, not a replacement
- Keep the changes minimal and focused
- The Workflow Triggers table format must remain valid markdown
